text: Your friend, Alex, has been missing for three days. The police have no leads. His last text was from the old, abandoned Crestwood Mall. You told him not to go.
You had to be sure. You had to look for yourself. The air inside is stale, heavy with the ghosts of forgotten commerce.
Deep in the service corridors, you find something... out of place. A section of wall that feels wrong. It's damp to the touch, and the wallpaper hums with a faint, almost imperceptible energy.
As your fingers make contact, the world dissolves into a deafening roar of static. The floor gives way. You are falling.
